rulings.Case Title: DONALD WOODARD VS. MOISES HERNANDEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Dismissal Filed 12/4/2025
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Monday, June 16, 2025 Line 1, PLAINTIFF DONALD WOODARD's Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Dismissal Filed 12/4/2025 Granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. The case is restored to the civil active list. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MATHILDE FROUSTEY VS. SAN FRANCISCO BALLET ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Tamara Rojo And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Monday, June 16, 2025 Line 3, PLAINTIFF MATHILDE FROUSTEY's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Tamara Rojo And For Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to compel the deposition of Tamara Rojo and for monetary sanctions is denied as to monetary sanctions and a hearing is required if the parties have not agreed on a date for Ms. Rojo's deposition. This motion should not have been filed since all issues relating to Ms. Rojoj's deposition should have been addressed on the Ballet's motion to stay Ms. Rojo's deposition. Two motions pertaining to a single deposition is a serious misuse of the court's limited resources, not to speak of the parties' own resources. The court is extremely troubled by the parties' inability to agree on a date for the deposition. The court urges the parties to give it one more try. If the parties have not agreed to a date by the time of the hearing, counsel will need to appear with their calendars and Ms. Rojo's calendar and the court will pick a date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL HENRY VS. ERIC YOUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp Section 128.7(H) Against Defendant Andrew Smith Lewis And His Attorneys Of Record Showning Poppler And Chan Punzalan Llp
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Monday, June 16, 2025 Line 9, PLAINTIFF PAUL HENRY , AN INDIVIDUAL's Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp Section 128.7(H) Against Defendant Andrew Smith Lewis And His Attorneys Of Record Showning Poppler And Chan Punzalan Llp. Defendant Paul Henry's motion for sanctions under CCP 128.7 is denied. Mr. Henry has not shown that Mr. Smith's sanctions motion was filed for an improper purpose. The court is extremely troubled by the parties' wasteful and expensive efforts to litigate the merits of this case through sanctions motions. Mr. Smith's request for sanctions is also denied as there was some basis for this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: JACOB BOULWARE VS. ABC TILE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay This Case Pending Silicosis Actions Case Petition For Coordination; Declaration Of Christine Brice
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Monday, June 16, 2025 Line 4, 1-DEFENDANT CAESARSTONE USA, INC. Motion To Stay This Case Pending Silicosis Actions Case Petition For Coordination. Defendant Caesarstone USA, Inc.'s motion to stay this case pending adjudication of the coordination petition is denied.The statutes governing coordination vest the coordination judge with the power to stay the proceedings. CCP 404.5 provides that "Pending any determination of whether coordination is appropriate, the judge making that determination may stay any action being considered for, or affecting an action being considered for, coordination." (Code Civ. Procedure section 404.5.) Though section 404.5 does not deprive the court of the authority to stay proceedings, it evinces the Legislature's intent for the coordination judge to decide whether cases are stayed pre-coordination. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Monday, June 16, 2025 Line 8, 4-DEFENDANT MICROSOFT CORPORATION, MOJANG AB.'s Motion To File Portions Of Declarations Of Ambika Kumar, Juliamarie Abbott, And Suzanne Fogarty Under Seal. Continued to August 18, 2025 by the parties' agreement per the representation of Kiley Grombacher in the declaration filed May 22, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)
